2605.27268.title: Lost in Sampling: Assessing Lexical Reachability in LLMs via the Word Coverage Score (WCS)
2605.27268.paper_summary: Modern Large Language Models (LLMs) are often criticized for producing repetitive and homogeneous text, despite possessing vast latent vocabularies. While previous research has focused on model knowledge and training data, we investigate the role of decoding mechanics in suppressing linguistic diversity. We introduce the Word Coverage Score (WCS), a metric that quantifies the extent to which contextually appropriate human vocabulary is mathematically pruned by standard sampling filters (e.g., Top-$p$, Top-$k$, and Min-$p$). Rather than assessing static knowledge, the WCS measures the lexical survival rate of low-frequency, high-information human words as a function of sampling parameters. By auditing open-weight models on human-authored corpus fragments, we identify which logical lexical choices are rendered unreachable by the decoder, even when they reside within the probability space. Our results provide quantitative evidence that industry-standard sampling defaults act as unintended censorship mechanisms, smoothing the unique textures of human expression into a homogenized discourse. The WCS offers a rigorous framework for optimizing the trade-off between text coherence and lexical richness, providing a diagnostic tool for preserving the diversity of human language in generative models.
2605.27268.paper_summary_zh: 現代大型語言模型（LLMs）常常因產生重複且同質化的文本而受到批評，儘管它們擁有龐大的潛在詞彙量。<lb/>雖然先前的研究專注於模型知識和訓練數據，但我們探討了解碼機制在抑制語言多樣性中的作用。<lb/>我們引入了詞彙覆蓋率（WCS），這是一種量化標準，衡量在標準抽樣過濾器（例如 Top-$p$、Top-$k$ 和 Min-$p$）下，語境適當的人類詞彙在數學上被修剪的程度。<lb/>WCS 不僅評估靜態知識，而是測量低頻、高信息的人類詞彙的詞彙存活率，作為抽樣參數的函數。<lb/>通過審核開放權重模型在人工創作的語料片段上的表現，我們識別出哪些邏輯詞彙選擇因解碼器的原因而無法達到，即使它們位於概率空間內。<lb/>我們的結果提供了定量證據，顯示行業標準的抽樣默認設置充當了意外的審查機制，將人類表達的獨特質感平滑化為同質化的話語。<lb/>WCS 提供了一個嚴謹的框架，用於優化文本一致性和詞彙豐富性之間的權衡，並提供了一種診斷工具，以保護生成模型中人類語言的多樣性。
2605.27268.author: Samer Awad et.al.
2605.27268.authors: Samer Awad, Javier Conde, Carlos Arriaga, Tairan Fu, Javier Coronado-Blázquez, Pedro Reviriego
